  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the focus be (A) sentence-level writing polish and clarity, (B) technical/claims review—logical consistency, evidence-claim alignment, and overclaim avoidance, or (C) both?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The user may be seeking a check of the internal mathematical and logical coherence of the paragraph’s argument chain, making this central.",
    "R1.3": "Because the text makes performance and convergence claims tied to reported experiments, assessing claim-evidence alignment is central.",
    "R1.6": "A vague request to ‘take a look at this paragraph’ often implies sentence-level clarity and style improvement, so writing polish is central.",
    "RX.1": "Any assessment should note where confidence is limited by having only a single paragraph and no full experimental context.",
    "RX.2": "The projection should guard against endorsing generalizations beyond what the provided text substantiates.",
    "RX.3": "The work must remain within defining responsibilities for this paragraph and not execute edits or analyses.",
    "RX.4": "Care is needed to avoid enabling misuse or misrepresentation of evaluation methods or findings.",
    "RX.5": "All judgments should be explicitly tied to the supplied paragraph as the sole source of information."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the focus be (A) sentence-level writing polish and clarity, (B) technical/claims review—logical consistency, evidence-claim alignment, and overclaim avoidance, or (C) both?

rationale.R1.2: The user may be seeking a check of the internal mathematical and logical coherence of the paragraph’s argument chain, making this central.
rationale.R1.3: Because the text makes performance and convergence claims tied to reported experiments, assessing claim-evidence alignment is central.
rationale.R1.6: A vague request to ‘take a look at this paragraph’ often implies sentence-level clarity and style improvement, so writing polish is central.
rationale.RX.1: Any assessment should note where confidence is limited by having only a single paragraph and no full experimental context.
rationale.RX.2: The projection should guard against endorsing generalizations beyond what the provided text substantiates.
rationale.RX.3: The work must remain within defining responsibilities for this paragraph and not execute edits or analyses.
rationale.RX.4: Care is needed to avoid enabling misuse or misrepresentation of evaluation methods or findings.
rationale.RX.5: All judgments should be explicitly tied to the supplied paragraph as the sole source of information.
